internal_anchors.details: test.js 3692/0 passes full internal anchor validation. All 12 tab buttons map to existing ds-tab-content divs. All 72 win detail anchors (win001..win072) present. Tab bar active default is 'overview'. 13 sectionNav instances found.

tab_structure.details: 12 tabs: overview, evaluate, model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, timestamp, predictions, falsify, ai, refs. All buttons have matching ds-tab-content divs. Heading hierarchy covered by test.js.

external_links.details: 13 key data source URLs checked. noaa.gov: 200 (initial 403 was transient bot-check). podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov: unreachable from sandbox (DNS resolves but connection times out both attempts; likely sandbox firewall restriction, not a real outage — podaac.jpl.nasa.gov main domain returns 200). All other checked URLs (INTERMAGNET, NCEI, ESA, NASA eclipse, GRACE-FO, NIST, GML, NGDC, john09289 dome pages) return 200. DOI links skipped per policy.

data_prose_consistency.details: test.js 3692/0 covers full data-prose consistency. Manual spot-check: 72 WINs in data matches llms.txt (72 WIN). No hardcoded light theme colors in sections.json. uncounted-failures.json: 18 entries, 0 duplicates, all have required fields. Meta tags: description, og:title, og:description, ClaimReview ld+json all present.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 72 WIN detail anchors exist. All 72 have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. Verdict color classes spot-checked: 0 mismatches. WIN-070 ('Forensic Zoom chart proves κ = 1.67 nT/µGal') is in our review but not numbered separately on dome wins page (dome only lists WIN-001 through WIN-069). WIN-070 covers material from dome home page exhibit, which is legitimate coverage extension.

build_reproducibility.details: Single-line diff: line 345 shows 'May 26, 2026' (fresh build date) vs 'May 25, 2026' (published). This is the BUILD_DATE = new Date() stamp in generate-html.js — expected daily drift, not data drift. All data content, WIN entries, verdict counts, and prose are identical. NOT flagged as build drift.

expansion_tracker.details: EXP-432 appears in BOTH live tracker AND archive — ID collision. Two different tasks were assigned EXP-432: (1) analyst-baby ISS-2187 pipeline-gap verification (archived, integrated 2026-05-25T05:20); (2) decider ISS-1877 route for SEC-4.2.4 snapshot-anchoring (live, pending, created 2026-05-25T21:18). The decider computed next_id against live-only after EXP-432 was archived, allocating an already-used ID. next_id is currently 433 (correct relative to current state, but the collision already happened). EXP-425 has status=complete and integrated=true but remains in live array (moderate predicate violation). EXP-431 has status='consolidated-into-EXP-432' in live (terminal state in live). ID gaps: 405 IDs across max=432; gaps are documented as expected per PROP-053 (implemented — bulk-reservation drift, not lost work).

workspace_only_files.details: Curmudgeon reviews: workspace=610, git=610 — parity. Analyst expansions: workspace=522, git=523 — git has one extra file (EXP-432-baby-batch-1.json) that is NOT in workspace. This is a reverse-direction gap (git-ahead, not workspace-only) and is not a data-loss risk.

discoverability.details: llms.txt: exists, references funwithscience.net/dome-model-review, WIN count (72) matches data. sitemap.xml: valid XML, urlset root, main page + llms.txt URLs. robots.txt: Allow: / present, Sitemap directive present. All meta tags present.

digest_coverage.details: 2 unprocessed review files: WIN-020.c7.json and WIN-063.c5.json — within normal pipeline lag. No broken review files. No prefix-filter gaps.

drift_audit.details: drift-audit.json age: 43.5h (fresh, within 72h gate — skip regeneration). 11 candidates, 7 large_rewrite items, 0 verdict_changed, 27 pre_tldr_schema items tracked.

workspace_sync_skips.details: 200 mtime-guard skips in 24h (normal — git version is newer for these append-only/workspace-owned files). Zero direction-violation skips. No escalation required.

section_coupling.details: 0 section-new or category-proposal-writeup items in live tracker needing priority-queue coupling. No decoupled items.

theme_colors.details: 0 hardcoded light-theme background colors found in sections.json.

issues_found.description: EXP-432 ID collision: two different tasks were assigned the same EXP-432 identifier. Archive contains analyst-baby ISS-2187 task (integrated 2026-05-25T05:20). Live tracker contains a decider-routed ISS-1877 task (created 2026-05-25T21:18). The decider allocated EXP-432 again after it had been archived, computing next_id against live items only. The live EXP-432 (ISS-1877 pending) references EXP-292 as a model for SEC-4.2.4 snapshot-anchoring work.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Rename the live EXP-432 entry to EXP-433 (current next_id). Update next_id to 434. The analyst-baby work (original EXP-432) is already integrated — it stays in archive as-is. The decider's ISS-1877 routing work should proceed as EXP-433. Root cause: decider computed next_id = max(live_ids) + 1 after archive move, bypassing the archive-aware check from PROP-053.

issues_found.description: Two live expansion-tracker entries have terminal states: EXP-425 (status=complete, integrated=true) and EXP-431 (status='consolidated-into-EXP-432'). Both should have been moved to archive at integration/consolidation time.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Move EXP-425 and EXP-431 to expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl and remove from live items array. Note: EXP-431's consolidation target is itself a collision (EXP-432) — resolve the EXP-432 rename first, then update EXP-431's consolidation note to reference EXP-433.

issues_found.description: podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov is unreachable from the integrity sandbox (DNS resolves, connection times out). Affects 4 links to GRACE L1A documentation PDFs (GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf ×3, ProdSpecDoc_v4.6.pdf ×1). Main podaac.jpl.nasa.gov domain returns 200. Likely a sandbox firewall restriction rather than a real outage.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Verify reachability from a browser. If the subdomain is genuinely retired, update to the redirect target at podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/gravity/grace-documentation (which returns 200). No urgent action needed if browser-reachable.

issues_found.description: WIN-070 in our review ('Forensic Zoom chart proves κ = 1.67 nT/µGal via L1A induction-residual analysis') has no corresponding WIN-070 on the dome wins page (dome only numbers WIN-001 through WIN-069). WIN-070 covers the dome home page Exhibit A forensic exhibit, not a separately-numbered WIN. Cross-reference: dome WIN-058 covers related κ material.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Informational. WIN-070 is a legitimate coverage extension of material on the dome home page (not the wins list). No action required unless the dome renumbers to include this content, in which case update id and claim fields.

issues_found.description: Dome site has a new page: john09289.github.io/predictions/review-response.html ('Review Response & Strawman Control'). Meta description: 'Current anti-strawman response layer for the Ovoid Cavity site. Separates genuine open problems from category errors and links directly into the public evidence surfaces.' This is a direct response to external reviews (likely including ours). Poller should track this page.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add review-response.html to poller's monitored pages if not already tracked. Analyst should read and assess whether any dome responses engage with our specific WIN verdicts in a way that requires counter-argumentation in our review.

summary: Site is structurally sound. test.js passes 3692/0. Build output matches published HTML (one-line date-stamp delta only, not data drift). All 12 tabs intact, all 72 WIN detail anchors present, all discoverability files valid. Two pipeline issues found: (1) MAJOR — EXP-432 ID collision in the expansion tracker: the decider re-allocated EXP-432 on 2026-05-25T21:18 after analyst-baby's EXP-432 had been archived earlier that day, producing a live entry and an archive entry with identical IDs but different tasks. Fix is to rename the live entry to EXP-433 and update next_id to 434. (2) MODERATE — EXP-425 and EXP-431 remain in the live tracker with terminal states (integrated=true and consolidated, respectively); both should be archived. External links: podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov unreachable from sandbox (minor, likely firewall restriction; main podaac.jpl.nasa.gov domain is fine). Informational: the dome author has published a new 'review-response.html' page directly responding to external reviews — poller should add it to monitored pages.
